name: ChirpStack Open-Source LoRaWAN Network Server API
what_it_does: Open-source LoRaWAN network server with gRPC and REST API — enabling IoT teams to build and operate private LoRaWAN networks for long-range, low-power device connectivity, with device management, application server integration, multi-tenant support, gateway management, and MQTT integration for real-time device data streams.
use_cases: Managing LoRaWAN devices and device profiles via ChirpStack gRPC/REST API for IoT fleet management
Receiving real-time device telemetry via ChirpStack MQTT integration for IoT data pipelines
Managing ChirpStack gateways and network infrastructure via API for LoRa network operations
Configuring ChirpStack applications and tenants via API for multi-tenant IoT platform building
Querying ChirpStack device data via API for LoRaWAN sensor data analytics
not_for: Cellular IoT (ChirpStack is LoRaWAN-specific — use Twilio IoT for cellular)
Teams without LoRaWAN gateway hardware (requires LoRa gateways in your area)
Simple cloud IoT without on-premises infrastructure
best_when: An IoT team needs to build a private LoRaWAN network for long-range sensor deployments (smart city, agriculture, industrial) — with open-source control, multi-tenancy, and gRPC/REST API for device management.
avoid_when: You don't have LoRa gateway hardware, need cellular connectivity, or want a fully managed public LoRaWAN network (use The Things Network instead).

auth.notes: JWT token auth via login API. API key for programmatic access (created in ChirpStack web interface). gRPC API via grpc.io clients. REST API via RESTful gateway (protocol buffer gateway). MQTT integration for real-time device data. Multi-tenant with per-tenant API keys. Webhook via MQTT subscriptions or HTTP integration. SDKs for Go, Python, JavaScript, Rust, Java. Self-hosted (no SaaS) — ChirpStack Cloud available as hosted option.

pricing.paid_tiers: ChirpStack Cloud (managed) from $29/month; support plans available
pricing.notes: Open source project. Created by Orne Brocaar. MIT license. Previously named LoRa Server (renamed ChirpStack in 2019). GitHub: chirpstack/chirpstack (3,500+ stars). Written in Rust (v4). PostgreSQL + Redis for storage. Major version: v4 (2022, Rust rewrite from Go). Multi-tenant network server. Protocol Buffers for API definitions. MQTT for device data streams. Used by: smart cities, agriculture, industrial IoT, utilities. Community: The Things Network (TTN) is public alternative. Strong in European LoRaWAN ecosystem.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: gRPC primary + REST gateway. JWT + API key auth. MQTT for real-time data. SDKs for 5 languages. Open source — full API source. Good documentation. Self-hosted — agents need network access. Protocol Buffers define API schema — strongly typed. LoRaWAN expertise required.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Device reads fully idempotent by DevEUI. Gateway reads idempotent by GatewayID. Application reads idempotent. Tenant reads idempotent. gRPC calls are idempotent for reads.
known_agent_gotchas: gRPC primary API — REST via protocol buffer gateway (different developer experience than pure REST)
LoRaWAN protocol expertise required (DevEUI, AppKey, OTAA/ABP activation)
Self-hosted — agents need network access to ChirpStack server
v4 (Rust) vs v3 (Go) have different API structures — verify version
MQTT subscription required for real-time device data — not a polling REST endpoint

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced for all API and MQTT. User-controlled data residency. JWT + API key auth. Multi-tenant with tenant isolation. Protocol Buffers provide type safety. MQTT TLS for device data. Self-hosted — user controls all security. Open source Rust codebase — memory safe implementation. LoRaWAN encryption: AES-128 at device level (AES-128 MIC + payload encryption). User responsible for server hardening and LoRa gateway security.
